6. We recommend that the Management take full opportunity on the application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, if any, for the purpose of one-time cleaning of PPE account balances of the LGU.

7. We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the 20% Development Fund. Separate financial statements such as Balance Sheet, Statement of Income and Expenses and Cash Flows should be prepared and presented as part of the schedules of the General Fund Trial Balance.

8. We recommend that management with the assistance of the MPDC and other key players in the decision making of the LGU to strategize so that projects identified have to be implemented within the year to address the needs of the community and to get the maximum value of fund allocated for a specific period considering the increasing prices of materials. Further, conduct actual studies for previously identified projects and determine if those are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, consider for their reversion to release the funds and make available to some meaningful use.

9. We recommend that management require the municipal assessor to fast track the inspection and assessment of the real properties so that income collection from the real property tax will increase that can benefit the local government unit.  Moreover, we require the Municipal Engineer to furnish copies of the building permits and certificate of occupancy to the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the prompt and timely assessment of buildings.

10. We recommend management to formulate strategies and policies that will ensure an efficient and effective collection system, such as prompt assessment of new structures and frequent tax mapping to identify structures that need to be re-assessed or to be assessed so that the collection efficiency targets will be achieved.

11. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive evaluate the necessity of hiring advisers/executive assistants and determine whether funds disbursed are commensurate to their actual accomplishments. Stop the hiring of advisers/Executive Assistants whose works are already performed by regular agency personnel.

12. We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling –up the vacant positions by hiring new employees or expedite promotion of deserving employees to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents as well as to provide equal job opportunities to the community.

13. We recommend that management prioritize and establish the GFPS to promote gender mainstreaming and initiate programs, projects and activities to respond the priority needs and address immediately gender –related issues.  Further, evaluate the performance of the contracted GAD Focal Person and if found to have satisfactorily delivered the tasks assigned, consider hiring and create a GAD office to ensure continuity and sustainability of the GAD programs.

14. We recommend that Management sufficiently appropriate all the mandatory positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 and expedite hiring for the vacant positions to properly institutionalize the organization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and have continuity in service and implementation of programs.

15. We recommend that the regular employees of the LGU refund the honorarium received in connection to their appointment as RCG and MBOC support staff. Henceforth, abide by the COMELEC guidelines in the disbursement of funds.

16. We recommend that the management direct the MLGOO to secure a written authorization from the Municipal Mayor for official usage of the vehicle outside office hours. Otherwise, leave the motorcycle issued to him in the garage provided for by the municipality after office hours and weekends to prevent the improper and unauthorized use of the motor vehicle.

17. We recommend that Management create the Inventory Committee to conduct on time the physical inventory of all its property, plant and equipment, and prepare and submit the RPCCPE not later than January 31 of the ensuing year, in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs Volume I. The Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee has to reconcile the balance of PPE per books with that of the inventory reports, verify and analyze the discrepancies, and prepare adjustments to the affected records.

18. We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due From and Due To Other Funds and make the necessary corrections/ adjustments.  Moreover, when the transactions are already identified, coordinate immediately with the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts immediately to show the correct balances of the Cash in Bank per fund.  Finally, please take note to immediately facilitate fund transfers when intra-fund payables are being set up to show accurate cash in bank balances for each fund.

19. We recommend that management provide evidences/justification that the contract with the media company is favorable to the government.  Henceforth, require the Bids and Awards Committee to comply with the requirements under the Negotiated Procurement before resorting to this alternative method of procurement to justify the need to procure thru this modality and ensure that the government obtains the most advantageous terms and price from probable sources.

22. We recommend that management has to see to it that disbursement of funds are supported with complete documentation as required in Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445. Also, submit additional documents to confirm the actual attendance to the event to substantiate the payment made in order to avoid suspension of the transaction.

24. In view hereof, we recommend that management prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions to enhance its services and attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents. 

         Further, personnel movement has to be effected in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry of new applicants for lower level positions.

25. The monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction & Management is not submitted before the 15th day of each month to the COA Auditor per Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 thus, hinders the timely review of the fund utilization.

26. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant cause the retrieval of the MOA between LGU and PO-end-user and prepare turn-over documents as evidence of transfer of accountability of the equipment. These documents will be the basis in effecting the following proposed adjusting journal entries to drop the assets not already in possession of the Municipality previously recorded as Marine and Fishery Equipment:

Dr. Government Equity                   ₱1,808,825.00                          
   Cr. Marine & Fishery Equipment           ₱1,808,825.00

27. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant exhaust efforts to retrieve the records or transaction documents of Due from LGUs and Other Receivables in order to confirm the existence and validity of the receivables. 

     In case of non-retrieval of   the documents, disclose the amount in the Notes to Financial Statements and further follow the procedural guidelines in writing off dormant receivables in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005.

28. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to coordinate again with the bank management on the bank reconciling items in order to resolve the issue, otherwise, refer the issue to a higher authority so that proper actions will be taken by the bank on the errors noted.

29. We recommend that management collect the liquidated damages due from contractor amounting to ₱34,207.26. Moreover, we recommend that the Municipal Engineer monitor project accomplishments, investigate and review thoroughly any request for time extension before recommending them for approval to avoid grant of extensions without valid cause that would result in the unnecessary delay in the completion of projects.

30. We recommend that management provide justification through written explanation or provide additional documentary requirement on this noted observation. Moreover, we recommend that they observe and be updated with new procedures, guidelines and advisories on procurement required by the GPPB.

31. We recommend that upon completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, proper disposal of all obsolete and unserviceable properties shall be undertaken pursuant to the instructions of Section 79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379-381 of RA 7160 and procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296.

33. We recommend that management direct the persons liable to effect the full settlement of the suspensions by submitting the required documentations completely in order to avoid eventual disallowance of these transactions. Further, settlement of audit disallowance is likewise recommended.

We recommend that, effective immediately, all officials/employees paying dues and attending seminars/ conferences conducted by leagues with registration fees should demand for a government official receipt to acknowledge the payment for proper accounting of collections.

1.8 We recommended that Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments in the books recognizing the cost of the foregoing assets paid from trust liabilities to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality as of December 31, 2023.  Henceforth, adhere to the prescribed accounting policies in the recording of transaction to avoid misstatements in the financial records of the municipality.

2.8 We recommended that Municipal Accountant adhere the provisions of Section 44 of IPSAS 12 and Section 121, Volume I of NGAs Manual for LGUs in the accounting of inventories.  Detailed inventory records (Supplies Ledger Card) for each inventory item shall be prepared and maintained to established accurate account balances of inventories.

3.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify the causes and differences of the reciprocal accounts - Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books.  Henceforth, conduct regular review of these reciprocal accounts, reconcile and adjust the differences accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of RPT and SET Receivables.  Henceforth, discounts on RPT and SET for advance payments shall be recognize following the above prescribed journal entries to present fairly the financial statements of the municipality.

5.14 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entry for the current year as suggested below to correct the income declared in the year-end financial statements to arrive at a reliable balance.
5.15 Henceforth, follow the   journal entries presented in the recording of advance payments of RPT/SET to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU as of a given date.

6.14 We recommended that management revisit the Municipal Ordinance and propose amendments in accordance with established laws, rules and regulations considering the 40% CRMC share of the EUF collections held in trust as revenue to increase their income and to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the prescribed accounting policies in the recording of transactions on the year they are incurred to avoid misstatements and misleading information in the financial statements.

8.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe proper and correct use of accounts which shall be in accordance with the prescribed Chart of Accounts to be able to maintain accurate and reliable accounting records, and to ensure that the financial statements of the municipality are fairly presented.

9.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor the cash advances for travel to remove or lessen the amount of unliquidated cash advances pursuant to Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-004.  We also recommended that management stop the grant of financial assistance to barangays unless the previously granted financial assistance were already liquidated.  Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation letters to private sectors and individuals identified as debtors and demand payment thereof.

10.6 We recommended that management submit a request for the authority to write-off the dormant accounts following COA Circular No. 2023-008.
10.7 Shall the request be approved, drop the dormant account from the books of accounts by drawing the following journal entry:
Government Equity					447,206.00
Advances to Officers and Employees			35,100.00
Other Receivables					 442,106.00			#

11.10 We recommended and Management agreed to create an Internal Audit Service (IAS) Unit that would handle internal control functions of the agency pursuant to the above-mentioned rules and regulations. Refer to the PGIAM and the NGICS as guide in strengthening the internal control system of the LGU.

1. We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) of Taxpayer’s Index Card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve at the basis in recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts in the books.  This is in view of the need to record in the books not mere estimates from the real property taxes but actual receivables from said taxes pursuant to Section 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1, in order to reflect in the books accurate amounts for RPT and SET Receivables.
2. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the Accounts Payable and asset accounts:

3. We recommend that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in locating the complete supporting documents on the Loans Payable and come up with the correct balance of loans to be presented in the financial statements, and disclose the details of the payable account in the Notes to Financial Statements.
5. We recommend that the management effect the increase of the bond coverage pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, to cover the accountability and to protect the interest of the government.
20. We recommend that management require the BAC Secretariat to observe the mandatory posting of all notices and contracts awarded pursuant to the provisions of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

21. We recommend that management immediately stop the practice of charging to SEF expenses which are not chargeable to the fund and observe the proper utilization thereof pursuant to the provisions of Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 and 2020 of DepEd, DBM and DILG. Moreover, require the refund of the expenses not chargeable to SEF.

23. We recommend that management require the refund of financial assistance amounting to ₱4,000.00 given to the contestants as the expense is not for public purpose. Henceforth, management has to ensure that all disbursements of public funds is for public purpose and to stop the practice of incurring expenses that are not responsive to the mission of the agency.

32. We recommend that the Municipal Engineer submit a justification for the delay in the implementation of the project for its desired purpose. We also recommend that the inventory committee submit an updated inventory report specifying the number of broken pots which are not usable anymore so that persons-in-charge may be held liable for this.

We further recommend that management plan all its procurement properly and effectively monitor its implementation so that government funds are not wasted otherwise, this may be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that management allocate a budget to address the problem identified in the use of the public toilets and strengthen their monitoring system to ensure that projects are completed and functional to avoid wastage of scarce government resources.  

We require management to submit explanation/s on the outcome of this project, its implementation even without stable source of water. Identify liable person/s responsible to address the problem and require explanation for failure to have the toilet functional/ be of public use.

Secure the place to prevent further damage

We recommend that Management justify the charging of the procurement of the school bus against the SEF.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) shall instruct the personnel-in-charge of the bus to check and assess its current condition. Assign a skilled driver if it is found safe and proper to use in order to maximize the benefits derivable from it. If it needs further repairs and maintenance, estimate the cost and have it conditioned if allowed by the present circumstances.  

We also recommend that the LCE order the processing of the documents necessary for the bus’ registration with the Land Transportation Office, if found to be procured in accordance with the guidelines on SEF disbursements.

We further recommend that the LCE shall henceforth plan all its procurement properly and effectively monitor them so that government funds are not wasted.

Comply with the prohibition set by the Local Government Code and the 1987 Constitution and immediately stop the payment of the services of the concerned losing candidates while the one-year prohibition on their hiring is still effective;

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that claims are duly supported with complete documents appearing legal and proper before effecting payments thereof.

Direct the Local Finance Committee and the Human Resource Management Office to determine the necessity of hiring advisers/executive assistants and vice versa.

We recommend that Management submit to the audit team an explanation for the absence of Contracts of Services with the consultants and for the latter to submit their individual curriculum vitae/resume and track record of accomplishments necessary to evaluate their eligibility following the eligibility check and post- qualification of consultants provided under Sections 21, 22, 24, 25, 33 and 34 of the same Revised IRR of R. A. No. 9184.

We require management to submit explanation/s on the outcome of this project, its implementation even without stable source of water. Identify liable person/s responsible to address the problem and require explanation for failure to have the toilet functional/ be of public use.

Secure the place to prevent further damage and loss of materials.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Finding #12 | The MEO has still yet to make necessary corrective actions.
observations.reason: No actions taken by the LGU

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management that the images of municipal officials be not displayed in LGU vehicles and the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 be faithfully adhered.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding #5 | The LGU would incur additional costs for the removal of the pictures
observations.reason: Pictures are still visible for old vehicles, however, newly purchased vehicles do not bear pictures of the Mayor and Vice-Mayor

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create an inventory and disposal committee to act immediately in the proper identification, ascertain existence, location and disposal of these assets in order to recover some monetary value of the properties and to fairly present the accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 | The preparation of inventory of the obsolete and unserviceable assets is still on going.
observations.reason: The management shall meet with the inventory committee to make proper actions on this matter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management adhere with the principles on transparency and competitiveness by conducting competitive public bidding in the procurement of consultancy services in accordance with Section 2 of the Revised IRR of R. A. No. 9184 and submit explanation for the non-adherence of the same in the hiring of the seven (7) Executive Assistants.

b. Duties and functions required from the Executive Assistants could be done by regular employees of the LGU making the disbursement unnecessary.

We recommend that Management:
1.Evaluate the need for the consultants viz-a-viz their respective expertise which skills are 	not possessed by the regular staff and based on the result of such review, consider the termination of the contracts when services provided are redundant and unnecessary; and
2. Submit to the audit team a justification stating the necessity of hiring a consultant, otherwise payment made will be disallowed in audit.

c. Other services required to be rendered by the Executive Assistants include supervision over regular employees rendering the disbursement irregular.
We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations in hiring a consultant. We also recommend that the irregular payments made shall be refunded by the personnel concerned.

d. Executive Assistants that are considered consultants by the municipality were not required to sign Contracts of Services making the disbursements pertaining thereto lacking in legal basis.

We recommend that Management submit to the audit team an explanation for the absence of Contracts of Services with the consultants and for the latter to submit their individual curriculum vitae/resume and track record of accomplishments necessary to evaluate their eligibility following the eligibility check and post- qualification of consultants provided under Sections 21, 22, 24, 25, 33 and 34 of the same Revised IRR of R. A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 | The management awaits the finality of the decision of the higher courts.
observations.reason: There is an on-going case relative to this Audit Observation

observations.recommendation: We recommend that, effective immediately, all officials/employees paying dues and attending seminars/ conferences conducted by leagues with registration fees should demand for a government official receipt to acknowledge the payment for proper accounting of collections.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 | The leagues are not using the government official receipts
observations.reason: The leagues explained that they are going to consume all of their ORs first.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management justify the charging of the procurement of the school bus against the SEF.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) shall instruct the personnel-in-charge of the bus to check and assess its current condition. Assign a skilled driver if it is found safe and proper to use in order to maximize the benefits derivable from it. If it needs further repairs and maintenance, estimate the cost and have it conditioned if allowed by the present circumstances.  

We also recommend that the LCE order the processing of the documents necessary for the bus’ registration with the Land Transportation Office, if found to be procured in accordance with the guidelines on SEF disbursements.

We further recommend that together with the procurement team, the LCE shall henceforth plan all its procurement properly and effectively monitor them so that government funds are not wasted.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 | The management waits for the result of the appeal.
observations.reason: ND was already served relative on this transaction. The LGU awaits the notice of finality on the appeal.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 | The current LCE felt the need for the position.
observations.reason: With on-going case in the Sandiganbayan

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant exhaust efforts to retrieve the records or transaction documents of Due from LGUs and Other Receivables in order to confirm the existence and validity of the receivables.

In case of non-retrieval of the documents, disclose the amount in the Notes to Financial Statements and further prepare a request for write-off from the Commission on Audit in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the bank to bank fund transfer for payroll using the following proposed journal entries:

(a) CIB-LCCA (PVB) 			xxxx
CIB-LCCA (LBP) 				xxxx
To record transfer of funds for payroll for the period
Jan 1-15, 20xx.
(b) Salaries & Wages – Regular		xxxx
CIB-LCCA (PVB) 				xxxx
To record payment of salaries & wages for the period
Jan 1-15, 20xx.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record the intrabank fund transfers and electronic remittances of mandatory government contributions using the following proposed journal entries:

(a) CIB-LCCA (LBP-special account)	xxxx
CIB-LCCA (LBP-GF proper)		xxxx
To record transfer of funds used for e-payment of mandatory contributions for the month of January 20xx.
(b) Due to BIR					xxxx
Due to Pag-ibig				xxxx
CIB-LCCA (LBP-special account)	 xxxx
To record remittance of withheld amount for the month of January 20xx.

We recommend that upon completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, proper disposal of all obsolete and unserviceable properties shall be undertaken pursuant to the instructions of Section 79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379-381 of RA 7160 and procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect the following proposed adjusting journal entries to drop the assets not already in possession of the Municipality previously recorded as Marine and Fishery Equipment:

Government Equity		1,808,825.00
Marine & Fishery Equipment			1,808,825.00

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace the transactions pertaining to the Due to NGAs account to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances and effect the necessary adjustments in order to reconcile the GL and SL balances and present reliable financial reports.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor submit an explanation for the conduct of benchmarking outside the geographical area when the same can be conducted in Cebu, so as to avoid disallowance of the additional transportation expenses incurred which could have been dispensed with had they observed the judicious and prudent use of government funds under AO No. 6.

Further, submit an explanation for the non-inclusion of the Municipal Treasurer or her representative in the delegation when the main purpose of the travel was to improve the revenue collection efficiency of the LGU.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer submit a justification for the delay in the implementation of the project for its desired purpose. We also recommend that the inventory committee submit an updated inventory report specifying the number of broken pots which are not usable anymore so that persons-in-charge may be held liable for this.

We further recommend that management plan all its procurement properly and effectively monitor its implementation so that government funds are not wasted. Likewise, management has to make action to make use of these fabricated pots otherwise, this may be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant submit an explanation for the release of confidential fund in excess of the allowable limit and for the delay in the submission of the vouchers and supporting documents to the auditor, respectively.

Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor must always ensure that provisions of Joint Circular 2015-01 on the release and liquidation of confidential funds are observed.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory committee and conduct the actual physical inventory of all properties of the LGU and simultaneously perform the property tagging.

We also recommend that the Accounting Office prepare the PPE ledger cards in order to verify the balances reported in the inventory report.

We recommend that the Human Resource Management Officer - Designate monitor the tardiness and undertime of the employees and deduct these from their earned leave credit balances to avoid overpayment of terminal leave benefits otherwise, these will be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Point take determined efforts to maintain GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation, in compliance with PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend that the HRMO-designate require the concerned department heads to submit activity designs, including project’s success indicators to substantiate and justify the necessity of hiring the JOs and payment of wages in their office. Further, the Municipal Accountant and HRMO must strengthen the internal control system in hiring of additional workforce through job orders and observe related laws and regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to judiciously and meticulously plan projects/programs/activities (PPAs) to be included in the Local Development Plan and Annual Investment Plan indicating in details specific PPAs of the 20% LDF in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people.

Further, the Municipal Engineer must conduct in-depth analysis and ensure that Program of Works (POW) have been prepared in order to ascertain a more realistic estimates of work items and costs as basis in appropriating fund thereof.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Person prepare and review carefully the GAD plan and budget pursuant to the existing rules and regulations. Ensure that allocation for GAD must be at least 5% of the current year’s budget.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018-06 | Allocation for GAD is equal to 5% of current year’s budget.
observations.reason: GAD budget was properly allocated in accordance to the provisions of JC 2004-1.

observations.recommendation: Submit explanation and cite legal basis of granting a fixed amount of RATA to the OIC – MPDC. Adhere to the provisions of DBM LBC Circular No. 103 for the amended rules and regulations on the grant of RATA to avoid irregular payment of allowances and benefits.

AAR 2017-14

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the management that:

a. Section 69, 71 and 72 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual shall be strictly adhered.

b. An Official Receipt – Accountable Form 51 shall be issued upon payment of the Balicasag Diving and Snorkeling fee by the payors.

c. The use of the ticket should be for control purposes only and never in lieu of the Official Receipt as this is not yet considered as an accountable form to acknowledge receipt of municipal collections.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Finding #2 | The management felt the need to issue the specialized tickets to divers and snorkelers as a token for the said activity. The said specialized tickets are for control purposes and not in lieu of the Official Receipt.

observations.recommendation:
a.)	Direct all concerned officials responsible in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

b.)	Demand for the immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advances from officers and employees who are     still    in    active service with the LGU and send demand letters/ tracers to officers and employees who no longer connected with the LGU but with known latest addresses within or outside the locality.

c.)	Details of the cash advances as to the dates granted should be specified in the aging schedule for cash advances to afford proper analysis and review.

d.)	After due notice to concerned officials/employees, deduct the amount of the unliquidated cash advances from the benefits due to officials and employees still in active service.

e)   Henceforth, do not issue clearances  to those officials and employees with unsettled cash advances who are retiring or resigning from the service

The accounting unit also ensures that no cash advances shall be provided to individuals who were not able to liquidate their previous cash advances.
observations.reason: Cash advances amounting to ₱370,360.91 remained unliquidated at year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:
1.Evaluate the need for the consultants viz-a-viz their respective expertise which skills are 	not possessed by the regular staff and based on the result of such review, consider the termination of the contracts when services provided are redundant and unnecessary; and
2. Submit to the audit team a justification stating the necessity of hiring a consultant, otherwise payment made will be disallowed in audit.

We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations in hiring a consultant. We also recommend that the irregular payments made shall be refunded by the personnel concerned.

We recommend that Management submit to the audit team an explanation for the absence of Contracts of Services with the consultants and for the latter to submit their individual curriculum vitae/resume and track record of accomplishments necessary to evaluate their eligibility following the eligibility check and post- qualification of consultants provided under Sections 21, 22, 24, 25, 33 and 34 of the same Revised IRR of R. A. No. 9184.

We recommend that Management submit to the audit team an explanation for non-undertaking a Contract of Service with the consultants and for the latter to submit their individual curriculum vitae/resume and track record of accomplishments thereby their eligibility could be evaluated following the eligibility check and post- qualification of consultants as contained under Sections 21, 22, 24, 25, 33 and 34 of the same Revised IRR of R. A. No. 9184.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the LGU administrator, treasury and accounting offices to adhere to COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 to properly account and control gasoline, oil and lubricants consumption, and use of motor vehicles. We also recommend the submission of the pertinent Monthly Fuel Consumption Reports and trip tickets to avoid disallowance of these transactions.
observations.managementAction: Driver’s Trip Tickets were already prepared and attached to the disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: Monthly Fuel Consumption Reports are not submitted to the Auditors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents of the identified transactions otherwise, these might be disallowed eventually.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant see to it that necessary documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before payment and that concerned local officials strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on documentary requirements in the disbursement of public funds, pursuant to Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445, among other pertinent provisions, to prevent loss of meager government resources.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant sees to it that necessary supporting documents are complete before payment is made.
observations.reason: There were few transactions for 2016 that lacks documents which were already suspended in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee abide by the provisions of R.A.9184 by requiring the rural health unit to prepare ahead of time, a comprehensive list of medicines and medical supplies to be used for its operations for its inclusion in the Annual Procurement Plan, in order to avoid the repetitive use of Shopping as the mode of procurement.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly public bidding was conducted for the purchased of medicines and medical supplies

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management justify the charging of the procurement of the school bus against the SEF.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) shall instruct the personnel-in-charge of the bus to check and assess its current condition. Assign a skilled driver if it is found safe and proper to use in order to maximize the benefits derivable from it. If it needs further repairs and maintenance, estimate the cost and have it conditioned if allowed by the present circumstances.  

We also recommend that the LCE order the processing of the documents necessary for the bus’ registration with the Land Transportation Office, if found to be procured in accordance with the guidelines on SEF disbursements.

We further recommend that together with the procurement team, the LCE shall henceforth plan all its procurement properly and effectively monitor them so that government funds are not wasted.

observations.recommendation:
a) Activate the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to mop out plans, projects and programs to address and respond risk reduction in times of calamities based on the actual calamities experienced by the LGU and from learning from other communities affected by disasters or calamities, including recovery programs;

b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Local Finance Committee to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines for the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) of the LGU as set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002 to properly provide Management with accurate financial data on available resources of Calamity Fund and its utilization to serve as guide in decision-making, and to prevent the incurrence of irregular, unnecessary and illegal disbursements of the Fund.

c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly analyze the accounting entries involved in taking up the receipts and transfers of Calamity Fund and to prepare and effect the necessary adjusting/correcting journal entries to reflect accurate financial data and reports.

d) 	 Advise the Municipal accountant to refer to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 as guide in the accounting entries of transactions involving the Calamity Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has not drawn an adjusting entry to effect the transfer of DRRM Funds to Trust Funds.
observations.reason: There was no proper turnover made by the Municipal Accountant- Designate to the newly appointed Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
a)Adhere to the guidelines and provisions of Section B, Part 5 of the Updated Budget Operations Manual for Local Government Units and Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of the DBM, NEDA and National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women in the preparation of GAD Plan. 

b) Appoint/designate a GAD Focal Chairperson to take charge of GAD related activities and programs.

c) Ensure that only GAD related/responsive programs, activities and projects should be included in GAD’s plan and disbursed out of the GAD fund allocation.

d)Require the Chairperson to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget and the Accomplishment Report based on the guidelines prescribed under DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal person sees to it that only expenses which were GAD related were funded out of the budget.

observations.recommendation:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the List of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) Purchased starting from 2011 and work backwards based on the cashbook.

b. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant – Designate to prepare the same list based on the Journal of Checks Issued.

c. Further, direct the Chief of Offices to list down the PPE found in their Office and their respective Memorandum Receipts/Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment. 

d. Create an Inventory Committee with the Municipal Treasurer as the Chairman to conduct physical count of PPE based on the her summarized  list of PPE from the different Offices.

e. Require the Accountant to reconcile the Inventory Report of all PPEs prepared by the Municipal Treasurer and make the necessary adjustments to ensure fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

f. Issue/renew the Acknowledgement Receipts (AREs) to cover all pertinent PPEs and or update the same.

g. Prepare a Depreciation Schedule based on the Inventory Report and draw Journal Entry Voucher for Prior Year’s Depreciation Expenses and Accumulated Depreciation using the following suggested entry:

Debit                     Credit

Prior Year’s Adjustment 		 	 	 xxx

Accumulated Depreciation
    (appropriate PPE)						 xxx
observations.managementAction: An inventory listing as of Dec 31, 2013 is available. No updated report has been submitted.
observations.reason: The schedule does not tie-up with the GL balances

observations.recommendation:
Direct all concerned officials responsible in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances.

Demand for the immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advances from officers and employees who are     still    in    active service with the LGU and send demand letters/ tracers to officers and employees who no longer connected with the LGU but with known latest addresses within or outside the locality.

c.) Details of the cash advances as to the dates granted should be specified in the aging schedule for cash advances to afford proper analysis and review.

After due notice to concerned officials/employees, deduct the amount of the unliquidated cash advances from the benefits due to officials and employees still in active service.
e)   Henceforth, do not issue clearances  to those officials and employees with unsettled cash advances who are retiring or resigning from the service
observations.managementAction: A demand from the Ombudsman was received by the officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: There are still unliquidated cash advances
